Implemented via WeakValueDictionary registry in workflow.py: live Workflow objects are weakly referenced, so abandoned sessions (e.g. unique channel_id per request) auto-evict on GC with no manual close(). Parent/child topology moved onto the live object (_children). Verified: identity preserved, context override on recreate, child topology + close() teardown, and GC auto-eviction (registry returns to 0). Added tests/test_workflow_inmemory_store.py (4 tests). Full suite: 477 passed; the lone failure was the flaky LLM-training routing test, which passes on retry.

Fixed: test_train_modern_stack.trained_hello_world now trains into an isolated temp copy (tmp_path_factory + shutil.copytree, excluding generated dirs) instead of the real fastworkflow/examples/hello_world, so _cleanup()'s rmtree(___command_info) no longer destroys the trained model other tests depend on. Verified: full suite 478 passed / 0 failed (previously 4 test_fastapi_service.py failures), and the real example's ___command_info/global/threshold.json survives a full run -> suite is now idempotent.
Added process_action_turn() -> TurnOutput in workflow_execution_context.py (mirror of process_turn for the direct-action path).

Built run_fastapi_mcp/turns.py: ExecState, TurnExecution, TurnRegistry (construction-order contract + has_active), submit_turn (wait-or-defer + shield), _run_turn (lock per attempt, persist before DONE), render helpers. Module-level turn_registry in __main__.
ensure_user_runtime_exists serializes creation per-channel via get_creation_lock + double-check; build logic extracted to _create_user_runtime.

Built run_fastapi_mcp/turns.py: ExecState, TurnExecution, TurnRegistry (construction-order contract + has_active), submit_turn (wait-or-defer + shield), _run_turn (lock per attempt, persist before DONE), render helpers.
ensure_user_runtime_exists serializes creation per-channel via get_creation_lock + double-check; build extracted to _create_user_runtime.
